prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit, PySCF RKS) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation (N−1): charge +1, multiplicity 2; Anion (N+1): charge −1, multiplicity 2. Fukui indices were computed via finite differences of Mulliken charges across the three species (neutral, cation, anion). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ index = 0.49641778 at atom 4 (ipso carbon, C attached to –NO₂), identifying it as the most electrophilic site (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack). The most nucleophilic site is atom 5 (ortho-C, f⁻ = 0.10729). Integrity: trustworthy = true; sum(f⁺) = 1.0000; sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No error warnings were reported by the engine. The three integrity checks all passed: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ (sum(f+) = 1.0000, should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge), fukui_charge_conservation_f- (sum(f-) = 1.0000, should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge), schema_header (common header present). Method: DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), PySCF, gas phase, Mulliken charges, 14 atoms.
